sprintGoal: Make MemClaw a faithful Memory organ inside a Loop Engineering loop — NOT the whole loop. The Loop Engineering working note (Loop-Engineering-IEEE.pdf / Orange Book) is explicit that loop engineering is 'a set of capabilities, not a product': the loop's six organs (automations=scheduling, worktrees=handoff, skills=discovery, connectors, sub-agents=verification, memory=persistence) live in the HARNESS (Claude Code: /loop, --worktree, SKILL.md, MCP, .claude/agents + /goal, on-disk state). MemClaw is exactly ONE of those organs: Memory/Persistence. The original draft of this sprint over-reached — it had the memory store grow a scheduler (propulsion cron), an invented Turn-Distance Cost metric, and an in-process subprocess evaluator, i.e. one organ colonizing three the harness already owns natively. Those are dropped (see metrics.droppedFromOriginalDraft). The ONE genuine, in-lane gap this sprint closes is the paper's central failure mode — the 'Nodding Loop' (§VI.A): an agent that grades its own homework. Today memclaw_procedure_record drives reliability purely from the self-reported outcome_type, and the validation_passed field it already accepts (mcp_server.py:~3098) is silently DROPPED. This sprint makes the harness's independent evaluator (the separate-agent reviewer of Fig. 3) able to record a VERIFIED outcome that MemClaw distinguishes from a merely CLAIMED one, so procedure reliability reflects real test results — closing the 'verification debt' the paper warns accumulates between 'runs' and 'right' (§VIII).

workItems.objective: Stop dropping verification provenance in memclaw_procedure_record. The handler already declares validation_passed (mcp_server.py:~3098) but never reads it — reliability moves purely on the self-reported outcome_type (the 'Nodding Loop'). Wire validation_passed through so an outcome carries whether it was independently VERIFIED, persist a small verified/unverified counter split on procedure_stats, and surface a 'verified_reliability' alongside the existing self-reported reliability_score. No new table, no subprocess execution, no evaluator agent inside MemClaw — the harness's reviewer agent runs the tests and reports the verdict here; MemClaw only remembers it honestly.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 029 adds two columns to procedure_stats: verified_success_count (integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) and verified_failure_count (integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0). No other schema change. Migration is additive and reversible (down() drops the two columns); runs cleanly on top of 028 and is a no-op on a second `alembic upgrade head`
common/models/procedure.py ProcedureStats gains verified_success_count and verified_failure_count mapped columns matching the migration

memclaw_procedure_record reads validation_passed: when validation_passed is True, the outcome ALSO increments verified_success_count (on success) or verified_failure_count (on failure); when validation_passed is False or None, only the existing success_count/failure_count move (current behavior preserved exactly — backwards compatible)

The response JSON gains verified_reliability = compute_reliability(verified_success_count, verified_failure_count) computed from the verified counters only, returned alongside the existing reliability_score; when there are zero verified outcomes verified_reliability is null (not 0.5) so callers can tell 'no independent verification yet' from 'verified and reliable'

core-api test: record success with validation_passed=None -> success_count=1, verified_success_count=0, verified_reliability=null (proves the self-reported path is unchanged); record success with validation_passed=True -> success_count=2, verified_success_count=1, verified_reliability reflects the verified counters; record failure with validation_passed=True -> verified_failure_count=1 and verified_reliability drops

Quarantine logic is unchanged in THIS task (still driven by the combined success/failure counts) — verified counters are additive telemetry only. Any change to let verified failures quarantine faster is explicitly deferred (see LE-02)

reasons: Purely additive: two integer columns + reading a parameter that is already in the tool signature but currently ignored
Existing self-reported path is preserved byte-for-byte when validation_passed is None/False — no behavior change for current callers
No subprocess execution, no new agent, no scheduling — stays entirely within MemClaw's Memory organ. Rollback: down-migration drops two columns; verified_reliability field is inert

model_after: core-api/src/core_api/mcp_server.py memclaw_procedure_record (~line 3089) — the handler that already accepts validation_passed and drops it
facts: validation_passed is ALREADY a parameter of memclaw_procedure_record but is never read — this task gives it meaning rather than adding new surface
verified_reliability is null (not 0.5) at zero verified outcomes so 'unverified' is distinguishable from 'verified-and-mediocre' — directly serves the paper's verification-debt signal
The harness's evaluator agent (.claude/agents/reviewer.md per Fig. 3) is what sets validation_passed=True after running tests; MemClaw does not run the tests itself
validation_commands: cd core-storage-api && alembic upgrade head && alembic current

workItems.objective: Write SPRINT-LOOP-ENGINEERING_SIGNOFF.md recording the scope correction (why the original 3-upgrade draft was dropped to a single in-lane item), the LE-01 outcome, verification evidence, and a Loop Engineering organ-map showing that MemClaw correctly occupies ONE organ (Memory) rather than attempting to be the whole loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Includes a 'Scope correction' section: the original draft proposed verify_run (subprocess sandbox), cost_audit (invented Turn-Distance Cost Factor), and schedule_loop (propulsion cron); all three were dropped because scheduling, isolated subprocess verification, and the evaluator agent are HARNESS organs the paper assigns outside Memory (cite Loop-Engineering-IEEE Table V + the 'capabilities not a product' thesis)

Task status line for LE-01 (DONE/PARTIAL/PENDING) with the pytest evidence: the three record-outcome cases (validation_passed None vs True-success vs True-failure) and the verified_reliability null-at-zero behavior

Loop Engineering organ-map table: lists all six organs, marks Memory as MemClaw's lane (memclaw_write/recall/evolve/lifecycle + now verified-outcome reliability), and marks the other five as harness-owned (with the Claude Code primitive that provides each) — making explicit that 100% loop coverage is NOT a MemClaw goal
Sprint close summary states the one capability delivered and lists what was deliberately NOT built and why

implementation_notes: Lead the signoff with the scope correction — the most important artifact of this sprint is the decision to NOT build five-sixths of the original plan
Organ-map must cite the paper so a future reader understands the boundary is principled, not arbitrary

warnings: This sprint is deliberately SMALL (one feature + signoff). That is the point: per Loop-Engineering-IEEE §XII 'a first loop is better small', and MemClaw is one organ of six. Resist re-expanding scope — the dropped items are dropped on principle, not for time.

LE-01 must preserve the existing self-reported reliability path EXACTLY when validation_passed is None/False. The whole value is that an INDEPENDENT verdict (from the harness evaluator) is now distinguishable from a self-graded one; do not let the two counters merge.

MemClaw must NOT execute tests, spawn the evaluator, or schedule anything. Those are harness organs. If a future reader is tempted to add a subprocess runner here, that is precisely the over-reach this sprint corrected.

parallelWorkstreams: LE-01 (verified-outcome reliability) is the only build item; LE-Z01 signoff follows it

loopEngineeringOrganMap.memory_persistence: MemClaw's lane — memclaw_write/recall/evolve/lifecycle + (this sprint) verified-vs-claimed outcome reliability on procedures
loopEngineeringOrganMap.automations_scheduling: HARNESS — Claude Code /loop, Cloud Routines, GitHub Actions. NOT MemClaw.
loopEngineeringOrganMap.skills_discovery: HARNESS — SKILL.md (MemClaw's Forge mints skill docs, but discovery/invocation is harness-side)
loopEngineeringOrganMap.connectors: HARNESS/PROTOCOL — MCP (MemClaw is itself reached over MCP)
loopEngineeringOrganMap.subagents_verification: HARNESS — .claude/agents/reviewer.md + /goal fresh-model stop check. MemClaw only RECORDS the verdict it produces.

droppedFromOriginalDraft: memclaw_verify_run (subprocess-sandbox evaluator inside the memory store) — DROPPED: the evaluator is a separate AGENT (Fig. 3), a harness organ, not a function in the Memory store. The in-lane sliver (recording its verdict) survives as LE-01.

memclaw_cost_audit + Turn-Distance Cost Factor (base_cost*2^(turn_distance/10)) — DROPPED: the formula was INVENTED; the paper's 'cost scales with turns survived' (§II.C) is motivational intuition for why verification matters, not a mechanism it proposes building. Instrumenting a fabricated metric is speculative gold-plating.

memclaw_schedule_loop + propulsion-ticket worker cron — DROPPED: scheduling is the harness's organ (/loop + Cloud Routines). The draft worker even punted payload execution back to the harness, making it pure duplication.
deferredToNextSprint: Letting verified failures quarantine a procedure faster than self-reported ones (a verified-failure could carry more weight in the quarantine rule) — deferred until LE-01's verified counters have real data to tune against
